description: Complete workshop package for a 4-hour hands-on session at Faber Electronics, Heerenveen. 10 participants with mixed technical levels learn to identify, map, and automate business processes. The package consists of four deliverables: a presentation slide deck (Canva) for the trainer, a landing page (central hub with agenda, checklist, and resources), a pre-built Make.com demo scenario, and a pre-built n8n demo scenario. The workshop language is Dutch with English fallback where needed.

userStories.title: Landing page — Static structure and responsive layout
userStories.description: As a workshop participant, I want to open the landing page on my laptop or phone so that I can follow along during the session.
acceptanceCriteria: Single HTML file with inline CSS and JS (no build step)
Responsive layout works on desktop (1024px+) and mobile (360px+)
Clean, neutral design — no specific branding required
Header shows workshop title, date (2026-03-05), and location
Sections: Header, Agenda, Checklist, Tool Links, Reference Material, Resources, Contact
Page loads in under 2 seconds on a standard connection
Dutch is the primary language; section headings have English subtitle where helpful

userStories.title: Landing page — Visual agenda / timeline
userStories.description: As a participant, I want to see the day's schedule at a glance so I know what's coming and when.
acceptanceCriteria: Displays all 7 blocks with time slots (0:00–4:00)
Visual timeline format (not just a table)
Distinguishes block types: discussion, hands-on, demo, exercise
Current/active block is visually highlighted (optional: based on time, or manual toggle)
Responsive — readable on mobile without horizontal scroll

userStories.title: Landing page — Interactive checklist with progress
userStories.description: As a participant, I want to check off each step of the hands-on build (Block 4) so I can track my own progress.
acceptanceCriteria: 7 checkable steps matching the build instructions from Block 4
Each step has a title and short description
Checkbox state persists via localStorage (survives page refresh)
Progress indicator shows "X/7 steps complete" and a visual progress bar
When all 7 steps are checked, a celebratory state is shown (e.g. confetti animation, congratulations message)
Reset button to clear all checkboxes

userStories.title: Landing page — Tool links section
userStories.description: As a participant, I want quick access to all tools used in the workshop so I don't waste time searching.
acceptanceCriteria: Links to: Make.com signup, Excalidraw, Google Forms, Google Sheets
Each link opens in a new tab
Brief one-line description per tool (in Dutch)
Links are displayed as clickable cards or buttons (not a plain list)

userStories.title: Landing page — Reference material section
userStories.description: As a participant, I want to revisit the automation checklist framework and Make.com vs n8n comparison after the workshop.
acceptanceCriteria: Automation checklist: displays the 4 criteria (Herhalend, Regelgebaseerd, Digitaal, Tijdrovend) with explanations
Make.com vs n8n comparison table (matching the brief's table)
Make.com terminology table (Scenario, Module, Connection, Trigger) with Excalidraw equivalents
Content is in Dutch

userStories.title: Landing page — Resources and contact section
userStories.description: As a participant, I want post-workshop learning resources and a way to contact the trainer for follow-up.
acceptanceCriteria: Links to: Make.com Academy, n8n documentation & community, Excalidraw bookmark
2-week challenge callout: "Automatiseer iets op je werk binnen 2 weken"
RocketFlow contact details for follow-up questions
Visually distinct from other sections (e.g. footer or card style)

userStories.title: Presentation slide deck — Full workshop slides in Canva
userStories.description: As a trainer, I need a complete slide deck covering all 7 blocks so I can guide the workshop professionally and participants have visual support throughout.
acceptanceCriteria: Created in Canva with clean, neutral, professional design (consistent with landing page assets)
Covers all 7 blocks with clear section dividers between blocks
Block 1 — Wat zijn processen & wanneer automatiseren? (3-4 slides): opener prompt, automation checklist framework (4 criteria), group scoring exercise instructions
Block 2 — Proces uittekenen in Excalidraw (3-4 slides): Excalidraw intro, guided order intake process diagram example, duo exercise instructions with color coding rules
Block 3 — Introductie Make.com (4-5 slides): core concepts table (Scenario/Module/Connection/Trigger mapped to Excalidraw), interface tour highlights, pre-built demo flow overview
Block 4 — Bouw de order intake automatisering (2-3 slides): setup checklist reference, stretch goal for fast finishers, troubleshooting tips
Block 5 — Blik op n8n (3-4 slides): what is n8n, side-by-side comparison with Make.com, "wanneer wat gebruiken" table
Block 6 — Eigen automatiseringskans identificeren (2-3 slides): individual brainstorm prompt, duo-sharing instructions with the two feedback questions
Block 7 — Afsluiting (2-3 slides): recap of the day's arc (denken -> tekenen -> zien -> bouwen), resources overview, 2-week challenge, QR code to landing page
Title slide with workshop name, date, location
Closing slide with contact details and QR code
Pause slides between Block 2-3 and Block 4-5
Primary language is Dutch; English subtitles where helpful
Exportable as PDF for backup (in case of internet issues)
Estimated 25-35 slides total

userStories.title: Make.com demo scenario — Order intake automation
userStories.description: As a trainer, I need a pre-built Make.com scenario that demonstrates the full order intake flow live during Block 3.
acceptanceCriteria: Scenario triggers on Google Form submission (Watch Responses)
Adds a row to a Google Sheet ("Orders") with mapped form fields
Sends a confirmation email to the form submitter with order summary
Sends a notification email to a "production team" address with order details
Form fields: Bedrijfsnaam, Contactpersoon, Email, Product omschrijving, Aantal, Gewenste leverdatum
Scenario runs successfully end-to-end with "Run once"
Documented: screenshot or export of the scenario for reference

userStories.title: n8n demo scenario — Order intake automation
userStories.description: As a trainer, I need the same order intake flow built in n8n for the side-by-side comparison in Block 5.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow mirrors the Make.com scenario: Form trigger -> Sheet row -> Confirmation email -> Notification email
Uses equivalent n8n nodes (Google Forms Trigger, Google Sheets, Send Email)
Workflow runs successfully end-to-end
Documented: screenshot or JSON export of the workflow

userStories.title: QR code generation
userStories.description: As a trainer, I need a QR code pointing to the landing page URL so participants can quickly access it.
acceptanceCriteria: QR code resolves to the public landing page URL
High enough resolution for print (300 DPI minimum)
Available as PNG and PDF for printing on table cards or embedding in slides
Tested: scanning with phone camera opens the correct page

userStories.title: Landing page — Canva design assets
userStories.description: As a trainer, I want polished visual assets (header image, section dividers, or social card) created in Canva to give the landing page a professional look.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one header/banner image for the landing page (workshop title + visual)
Consistent visual style across assets (clean, neutral, professional)
Assets exported in web-optimized formats (PNG/SVG)
Assets embedded or referenced in the landing page HTML
